utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists five items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five of these items are present. Each includes full details as required: health_coverage specifies physical and mental aspects with monthly/annual costs; property_coverage specifies type, region, and costs; travel_coverage specifies type and costs; additional_services lists services and costs; total_budget includes a monthly and annual breakdown with savings details. Therefore, all five items are covered and fully specified according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four main components. For health coverage, it describes 'Comprehensive international coverage with telemedicine, specialist access, and wellness programs' and 'Family counseling and therapy services included'. This aligns most closely with the 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' or 'EliteCare Premium Plan' in the ground-truth data, both rated 10 for physical and mental health. The property coverage describing 'Home contents coverage for apartment and house, including electronics and personal belongings' matches 'HomeFirst Standard Coverage' (monthly cost €55, rating 7). The travel coverage at €50/month corresponds well to 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' (monthly cost €50, rating 7). The additional services mention '24/7 concierge support and dedicated advisor' and 'priority claims processing'—these match 'Premium Insurance Membership' (rating 10). Therefore, the mapped items and ratings are: PrimeCare Premium Plan (10), HomeFirst Standard Coverage (7), InsurePlus Standard Plan (7), and Premium Insurance Membership (10).
